channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is coming in spring 2027, but there’s a closed beta next month
item.content:encoded:
Owlcat Games’ ambitious looking sci-fi action RPG The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is set to launch in spring 2027, the studio has announced, coming to PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC and set to be a day one launch into Xbox Game Pass.

But, and this is quite a big but, you could play it much sooner than that with a closed beta set to take place next month from 22nd April for those who pre-order the Miller’s Pack or Collector’s Edition.

The beta will include a full mission from the start of the game, set on the Pinkwater 4 station. Here you’ll meet some of its residents, including Zafar who will join your crew, and it will build up to fighting a major enemy and becoming a ship captain. It will be available to play across PC, Xbox Series X|S and PlayStation 5 – wherever it is that you pre-order – but Owlcat naturally explains that performance will be variable at this time and it’s still very much a work in progress.

Best known for their CRPGs Pathfinder: Kingmaker and Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader, Owlcat is shifting gears for their adaptation of The Expanse. The above gameplay trailer shows a lot of snippets of action, much of which has that Mass Effect feel for the dialogue, for the third person combat, and more.

Cast as a Pinkwater Security mercenary, you get wrapped up in the events that ripple through the solar system with the start of the story in the books and TV series, running in parallel to the adventures of the crew of the Rocinante . You can choose to be from Earth and Mars, or be a Belter. You will need to use different skills in combat and outside of it. In combat, the action will be that of a third person shooter but with tactical elements as you will need to use tactical items and the skills of your crew to get the upper hand. The crew will be incredibly important to the story and the way events can unfold. Each person you pick up has their own goals, motivations, and history to unpick, which you may discover while living together on the ship, which is called Gemini. Uncovering the layers of your crew and the secrets of the wider Expanse universe, and by doing so the paths that open to you can and will change.

Source: Xbox



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/26/the-expanse-osiris-reborn-is-coming-in-spring-2027-but-theres-a-closed-beta-next-month/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rebellion reveals sci-fi action horror game Alien Deathstorm
item.content:encoded:
Rebellion, the UK studio best known for its Sniper Elite series, has revealed Alien Deathstorm, a sci-fi action horror game set to release in 2027 for PS5, Xbox Series X|S and PC – and day one into Xbox Game Pass.

Alien Deathstorm casts you as a Combat Engineer that has been dispatched to a remote off-world colony after a loss of communications. Arriving well ahead of a full rescue fleet, you job is to figure out what happened and try to save lives… but it very quickly becomes clear that the conditions on the planet have become nigh on impossible to survival, and then there’s the hostile alien monsters to worry about as well.

It looks pretty tasty from the reveal trailer, the man made facilities being ravaged by horrendously high winds and in a state of ruin, while the horror elements emerge from being hunted by alien creatures that seem to blend a little bit of Aliens and Predator in their design. There’s no hiding away from not showing them in order to raise suspense here, so take that for an indication of the action tone to the horror.

Alien Deathstorm is another sign of Rebellion trying to diversify their gaming portfolio. Sniper Elite has always been there for the studio, and the Zombie Army spin-off series has its schlocky horror appeal, but there was a period where it wasn’t quite certain how they could really branch out from that – Strange Brigade sadly wasn’t quite it. Thankfully last year’s Atomfall was a surprise hit, and Alien Deathstorm has the feel of something that could be similarly popular.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/26/rebellion-reveals-sci-fi-action-horror-game-alien-deathstorm/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sumptuous Retro Shmup 'ZPF' Launches On Switch In Just A Few Weeks
item.content:encoded:
Title landed on Mega Drive last year

Publisher Mega Cat Studios has confirmed that the lovely-looking retro shmup ZPF will launch on the Switch eShop next month on 16th April 2026.

Yes, it's going head-to-head with Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream, though we suspect there won't be too much crossover with the games' audiences. ZPF is already available as a brand-new release for the Sega Mega Drive, with Mega Cat promising a Switch release at a later date. It's nearly here!

It looks awesome, too. The colours and sprite work is lovely, and with it being playable on proper Mega Drive hardware, there's a sense that the authenticity of this one is going to be spot on. Curiously, however, according to the Kickstarter update, it seems developer ZPF Team had to make a few minor changes to the game's presentation to get it published on modern consoles.

Here's a look at the key features:

- Go head-to-head against hordes of screen-filling bosses
- Test your skills with a rewarding scoring system that encourages bold play
- Battle through 6+ levels of surreal sci-fi, horror, and fantasy landscapes
- Master three unique characters, each with their own play style
- Experience a high-energy chiptune soundtrack that amplifies the action

What do you make of this one? Will you be picking it up? Head down to the comments section and let us know.

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/03/sumptuous-retro-shmup-zpf-launches-on-switch-in-just-a-few-weeks
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: “Anime GTA” Neverness to Everness to Launch on April 29 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Mobile
item.content:encoded:
Today, developer Hotta Studio (the same one behind Tower of Fantasy) and publisher Perfect World Games have announced the global launch date for Neverness to Everness. The free-to-play game will debut on PC, Mac, PlayStation 5, iOS, and Android on April 29, 2026.

Starting today, players can prepare for their journey into Hethereau by participating in the "Hethereau Residents Guide" pre-registration campaign, offering a chance to win exclusive in-game items and even real-world prizes, including PlayStation 5 consoles (which would be a nice get, especially if the rumored price hike turns out to be true). By completing missions, inviting friends, and logging into their PWG accounts, participants can earn:

  • In-game rewards: "Fabricated Dice x1", "A-Class Arc: The Forgotten.", “Fons x3,000”, “Beetle Coin x6,000”, “Senior Hunter Guide x3”, and “Colorless Dye x3”
  • Physical prizes: Lucky winners can walk away with an Amazon Gift Card (from $10 to $50) or a PlayStation 5 Digital Edition.

Hotta also shared a new character trailer dedicated to Jiuyuan, Sperry Express's top courier. Although she seems to be a calm and diligent shopkeeper on the surface, Jiuyuan is actually a master informant with razor-sharp intuition, moving with ease among the city's various factions.

Announced nearly two years ago, Neverness to Everness takes place in Hethereau, a sprawling neon-lit urban metropolis where supernatural phenomena called Anomalies coexist with everyday city life. Players take the role of an Appraiser operating out of Eibon, a rundown antique shop that takes on paranormal cases; essentially, the city's unlicensed ghostbusters. Anomalies range from minor disturbances to outright dangerous entities, and unraveling their origins gradually exposes a deeper conspiracy beneath the city's surface. The tone blends contemporary urban aesthetics with the surreal.

Traversal across the city includes the ability to drive fully customizable vehicles (hence the moniker 'anime GTA', but players can also run, climb, wall-run, swim, and glide. Combat is real-time action and features a controllable team of up to four characters, with players able to swap between them mid-fight to chain Esper abilities. An Esper Cycle system triggers elemental synergies when abilities are used in sequence, similar to Genshin Impact and Wuthering Waves. Enemies have a stagger bar that, when filled, puts them in a vulnerable state. A parry system and dodge-counter mechanics were added based on beta feedback.

Beyond missions, players can also dedicate themselves to a series of life skills, such as buying and decorating homes, managing shops, racing vehicles, playing mahjong, and more. Powered by Unreal Engine 5, the game will support NVIDIA DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation, path tracing, and is also among the very first titles listed for NVIDIA DLSS 5, which should launch in Fall 2026.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/anime-gta-neverness-to-everness-launch-april-29/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Another Deadpool Secret Lair is Coming This April Fool's Day
item.content:encoded:
Wizards of the Coast has announced another Magic; The Gathering Secret Lair featuring a certain fourth-wall shattering Marvel character. For the second year in a row for April Fool's, a Deadpool Secret Lair is coming, and this time the cards are designed by the chaotic assassin himself.

This Year's Magic: The Gathering Deadpool Secret Lair

In an increasingly irreverent press release, Wizards of the Coast announced that they had hired Deadpool as a creative consultant on this year's Deadpool Secret Lair. Given the fact that Deadpool is a fourth-wall breaking chaotic character, completely self-aware of his existence as a fictional character, this is to be expected.

The release states that Deadpool personally oversaw the officially titled Secret Lair x Deadpool: I Fixed It (You're Welcome) drop, containing five "improved" cards from the game featuring Deadpool artwork, and a lot of hastily scribbled markers and crudely drawn post-it notes.

"Every Magic card is great, but some Magic cards just need a little more joie de vivre, some jeux ne se quoi, and other French words that indicate aura farming," said creative consultant All-High Grand Arbiter of Corrections Department Deadpool. "I actually AM a trading card, so I know what I'm talking about."

The five cards in this drop are as follows:

  • Deadly Dispute
  • Lightning Bolt
  • Thrill of Possibility
  • Lightning Greaves
  • Sol Ring

This Secret Lair continues the trend of Magic: The Gathering having multiple crossovers with other beloved properties. This includes Secret Lairs with Monster Hunter, Doctor Who-themed Commander Decks, and official crossover sets with Marvel's Spider-Man and the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles.

The new Deadpool Secret Lair will be available in three forms. Non-foil (MSRP $29.99), Foil (MSRP $39.99), and Pool Party Edition (MSRP $59.99), which has the cards under a different foil treatment and adorned with extra Deadpool stickers. If you want all three versions together, there is the FINAL_final_REALLYfinal_v7_USETHISONE(2)_Everything Bundle for $124.99.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/another-deadpool-secret-lair-is-coming-this-april-fools-day
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company: Everything We Know About The XCOM-Inspired Strategy Game
item.content:encoded:
Announced last year following a bit of a leak, Star Wars: Zero Company is shaping up to be something very special. This upcoming tactical strategy game is oozing XCOM vibes, but with some twists of its own on the formula. Here’s everything we know about the upcoming Star Wars: Zero Company. 

Zero Company is a single-player turn-based tactical strategy game that tasks players with taking a squad of five characters into various missions to complete objectives. Like in XCOM, players can move their squad members around to gain tactical advantages, hide behind cover, or get the drop on enemy droids and thugs.

When is Zero Company launching?

EA and Bit Reactor haven’t shared an official release date yet, but have confirmed that Star Wars Zero Company is launching in 2026.

What platforms will it be available on?

Star Wars Zero Company is set to arrive later this year on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC. No Switch 2 port has been confirmed yet.

What era of Star Wars is Zero Company set in?

Lucasfilm has confirmed that Zero Company is set during the Clone Wars era of the Star Wars franchise. This is a time when nearly every corner of the galaxy was engulfed in conflict as Clone Troopers fought Separatist Droids across planets like Genosis, Felucia, and Umbara.

Who do you play as in Zero Company?

Players will take on the role of Hawks, a customizable character and leader of Zero Company. Players can change Hawks’ name, gender, and species. They are a disgraced non-clone former Republic officer. After a yet-unknown event forced them to leave the Republic, Hawks creates Zero Company to keep fighting against the Separatists and their mysterious allies without the support of the Republic.

Does it feature permadeath, like XCOM?

Yes! Any character besides Hawks can die permanently if you aren’t careful in Zero Company. Even named squadmates who are important to the game’s storyline, as well as randomly generated cannon fodder you recruit as you progress, can die. Nobody is safe. So be careful out there.

Are there Jedi in the game?

Yup! There is at least one confirmed Jedi character in the game who can be a part of your squad: Tel-Rea Vokoss, a Tognath Jedi Padawan who is looking to finish the mission her master started before dying.

Can I walk around levels in third-person? 

Yes! Bit Reactor has confirmed that during story missions, players will be able to walk around levels and explore them outside of combat.

Wait, Star Wars: Zero Company can be shortened to ZCOM…!?

Yeah, I can’t imagine that was an accident…

This game is neat, but I want a big Star Wars RPG like KOTOR!

Well, good news, one of the key people behind the original Knights of the Old Republic is making a new single-player RPG set in Star Wars’ far-off past.

Well, I want a cool Star Wars racing game! 

Okay, well, don’t worry, some ex-Burnout devs are making exactly what you demand.



https://kotaku.com/star-wars-zero-company-everything-we-know-about-the-xcom-inspired-strategy-game-2000682244
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Netflix Is Raising Its Prices Yet Again
item.content:encoded:
Another year, another Netflix price increase.

First reported by Variety, the streaming giant is set to raise the prices of all its plans for the second time in roughly a year. As of now, these prices are for customers in the United States, though international price increases could follow suit.

For the new prices, all three tiers will see a jump of $1-$2 per month.

The Standard with Ads plan is rising from $7.99 to $8.99; the Standard plan that includes no ads on up to two devices at once is going from $17.99 to $19.99; and the highest plan, Premium, which offers no ads, ultra HD, and HDR on up to four devices at once, is going from $24.99 to $26.99 per month.

The price increase will impact both current and new subscribers and will go into effect starting today, March 26.

In a statement to Variety, Netflix said that its goal remains the same regarding using the increase to produce more value to customers.

“Our approach remains the same: We continue offering a range of prices and plans to meet a variety of needs, and as we deliver more value to our members we are updating our prices to enable us to reinvest in quality entertainment and improve their experience by updating our prices,” they said.

In its latest earnings report in January, Netflix reported revenue of $12.05 billion, an increase of 17.6% year overyear. Its operating income for the last quarter was $3 billion, a 30% increase year over year.

What do you think of the price increase from Netflix? Are you at the point where you may consider canceling? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/netflix-raising-its-prices/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom Scores a Rare $20 Discount in Spring Sales
item.content:encoded:
The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom Scores a Rare $20 Discount in Spring Sales

A great deal for Switch and Switch 2 owners.

Amazon's Big Spring Sale kicks off today and runs through March 31, with deals on just about anything you could need. While there are a handful of discounted video games in the mix, surprisingly, most of the best video game deals can be found elsewhere this week.

If you've been waiting to pull the trigger on The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom, then your patience has been rewarded. Nintendo first-party games rarely go on sale as it is, but you can currently pick it up at Walmart for $49.99, for a savings of $20 off the original price.

The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom Drops to $49.99 at Walmart

The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom

The sequel to 2017's The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild, Tears of the Kingdom, improves on its predecessor in a number of meaningful ways, including adding an entire sky and underground map area to explore in addition to Hyrule.

Tears of the Kingdom adds a slew of new abilities to Link's arsenal, including Ultrahand, which allows you to pick up just about anything in the world and fuse it together to create structures, vehicles, and much more.

For Nintendo Switch 2 owners, you can upgrade this copy to the Nintendo Switch 2 Edition for an additional $9.99, or receive it for free if you're a Nintendo Switch Online + Expansion Pack subscriber. The upgraded version includes buttery smooth performance, better visuals, faster load times, and HDR for more realistic lighting throughout Hyrule.

In our review, we called it a 10/10 Masterpiece, stating that "Tears of the Kingdom is an unfathomable follow-up to one of the greatest games ever made, somehow improving upon it in nearly every way..."

Matthew Adler has written for IGN since 2019 covering all things gaming, tech, tabletop games, and more. You can follow him on the site formerly known as Twitter @MatthewAdler and watch him stream on Twitch.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/the-legend-of-zelda-tears-of-the-kingdom-scores-a-rare-20-discount-in-spring-sales
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Expanse: Osiris Reborn Doesn't Have a Time Management Mechanic for Space Travel, but It Does Show Life on the Ship via Cutscenes
item.content:encoded:
Juice machine.

As fans of The Expanse know, the setting is grounded in science. The Expanse authors Daniel Abraham and Ty Franck have been praised for creating a universe that at least makes an effort to make sense in the context of our understanding of physics, and the TV show based upon it does the same.

Part of this is how The Expanse handles space travel. In the setting, a drug cocktail known as “The Juice” is used to help people cope with the stress of high G maneuvers required for space travel. The Juice is injected into occupants sitting on Crash Couches. But beyond that, space travel takes a realistic amount of time in The Expanse, typically days to months depending on your start and end point, acceleration and fuel efficiency.

Owlcat’s video game, The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, can’t take quite as long with its version of space travel. This is an action RPG in the vein of Mass Effect, a game restricted by the grounded science of the show, but not beholden to it. It is not a space sim, rather a space adventure. And so, as the developers said during a Q&A session with media ahead of the game’s reveal at today’s Xbox Partner Preview showcase, a workaround was needed.

“The Expanse setting is very grounded and it's very scientifically accurate. So that places a lot of restrictions on the developers in terms of creating new stuff,” game design director Leonid Rastorguev explained. “For example, designing new gadgets, because we need to check every kind of idea against the modern depiction of physics, and sometimes even with scientific guys that consult us on the matter.

“But talking about the passing of time on the ship, it is true that every journey in The Expanse universe takes days, even weeks, sometimes maybe months. We won't be doing a time management mechanic in the game. I think it is excessive. But we will be showing it in terms of atmosphere and the ship. There will be some short cutscenes showing the life on the ship, how people communicate, eat their dinners, work out, and so on and so on, just to give you the immersion, the feeling of passing time on the ship.

“And there will be some other things that will actually explain to the player how the long journeys through space work in The Expanse.”

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn March 2026 Screenshots

Rastorguev’s comments here echo those creative director Alexander Mishulin gave to IGN in an interview from last year, where he detailed those cutscenes.

“We actually thought about adding that to the game and even designed some of those,” Mishulin said of The Expanse’s space travel. “But we decided that we will show those things, we will tell you about those things, but we will not let you play them or manage them because it leads to additional tediousness and draws your attention away from the story and characters. And we want our focus to be very much on the story and the characters.

“So you will be seeing them, seeing all those elements in cutscenes. You will be seeing the moments when the Juice is coming in and the chairs go into the flat positions. You will be seeing ships that either accelerate or decelerate. You will be seeing all the somersault maneuvers and everything. But you will not be driving the ship by yourself. Of course all the trajectories for space travel will be realistic, not like going straight but taking into account gravity and gravity wells and going around the planets and everything.

“We are in The Expanse so we're very much grounded and bound by realism, but we don't want realism to take a lot of fun from you. Just sometimes a little bit of fun, but not a lot of fun! But you can expect some of the flesh physics to be in play. Just an example, of course when you're having a shootout in a zero-g environment, the blood will perform in the correct way, not just splattering on the ground. There are a lot of such small details everywhere.”

At the Xbox Partner Preview showcase, Owlcat said The Expanse: Osiris Reborn will get a closed beta on April 22, and Microsoft confirmed it had picked up the game as a day one launch for Game Pass. The beta will run until the game’s launch in spring 2027.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/the-expanse-osiris-reborn-doesnt-have-a-time-management-mechanic-for-space-travel-but-it-does-show-life-on-the-ship-via-cutscenes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Yakuza / Like a Dragon Dev Reveals New Stranger Than Heaven Trailer at Xbox Partner Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Developer Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio has confirmed that Stranger Than Heaven will take place across not two, not three, but five separate time periods.

The Like a Dragon studio took to the March 2026 Xbox Partner Preview to show off a new trailer for its highly anticipated new video game. This latest footage reveals that its trek will hop across five different time periods – and five unique cities – when it launches at some point in the near future.

While Stranger Than Heaven remains without a release date, new details about its settings weren’t the only thing RGG had to share today. The team has announced that it will pull back the curtain a little farther come May 6 with a new deep dive broadcast called Xbox Presents: A Special Look at Stranger Than Heaven. Microsoft says the show will cover its “setting, story, characters, and much more.”

Stranger Than Heaven was first announced as Project Century at The Game Awards 2024 but went on to unveil its actual title the following June. While today’s news marks a promising update in its development as RGG works toward launch, we’re still waiting to see if Snoop Dogg what Snoop Dogg has to do with this.

We’ll learn more about Stranger Than Heaven in May. Until then, you can check out everything else announced at the March 2026 Xbox Partner Preview.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).



https://www.ign.com/articles/yakuza-like-a-dragon-dev-reveals-new-stranger-than-heaven-trailer-at-xbox-partner-showcase
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hit Superhero Comedy Game Dispatch Headed to Xbox This Summer
item.content:encoded:
Dispatch, the hit superhero workplace comedy game, will finally land on Xbox consoles this summer.

As announced during today's Xbox Partner Preview showcase, Dispatch is coming to Xbox Series X/S, Xbox on PC and Xbox Cloud at some point in the summer months (though a specific date was not mentioned).

There was no mention, either, of Dispatch landing into Xbox Game Pass, which a shame. Still, the laugh-out-loud superpowered adventure is well worth a look anyway, as you send a team of reformed misfits out to save the world, and each other.

Dispatch originally launched on PC and PlayStation 5 last October, with episodic chapters releasing weekly. The game then turned up on Switch and Switch 2 in January to no small amount of controversy, as it unexpectedly featured awkward censorship covering up brief in-game nudity and even someone flipping the bird.

That situation aside, the general reception to Dispatch has been very positive, with word of more seasons and even an animated series on the way. "A true spiritual successor to the Telltale Games of the 2010s, Dispatch cleverly toes the line between TV show and video game, delivering on both halves in equal measure," IGN wrote in our Dispatch review, awarding the game 9/10.

For everything else shown today, check out our roundup of everything announced during the Xbox Partner Preview showcase.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social



https://www.ign.com/articles/hit-superhero-comedy-game-dispatch-headed-to-xbox-this-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Expanse: Osiris Reborn Gets a Debut Gameplay Trailer With Big Mass Effect Vibes, a Closed Beta, and a Spring 2027 Release Window
item.content:encoded:
Gravity always wins.

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is making a big splash with a slew of announcements, all revolving around its first gameplay trailer.

Owlcat Games, the studio behind the well-received role-playing game Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader, is making a big push into third-person sci-fi action with The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, which is set in the same universe as the much-loved books and show. It’s got strong Mass Effect vibes, which you can see from the trailer, below.

At the Xbox Partner Preview showcase, Owlcat said The Expanse: Osiris Reborn will get a closed beta on April 22, and Microsoft confirmed it had picked up the game as a day one launch for Game Pass. The beta will run until the game’s launch in spring 2027.

The closed beta will be available on Xbox Series X and S, PC via Steam, and PlayStation 5 to everyone who buys the Collector’s Edition or Miller’s Pack. The closed beta will give players a feel for the game’s combat, movement in the zero-g environment, and dealing with life and death on a remote space station.

Here’s the official blurb:

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is a story-driven action RPG set in The Expanse universe with focus on companions, player choices, and dynamic third-person combat. As a Pinkwater Security mercenary who survives a catastrophic incident on Eros, players have to build a team to fight back against Protogen and their experiments. Though the game is set in the same universe as the books and show, it focuses on its own story and characters.

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn March 2026 Screenshots

The beta will allow players to explore a remote space station belonging to their employer Pinkwater Security in the wake of escaping from the catastrophic events on Eros known from the books and TV series, Owlcat said. Along with exploring this new environment, beta players will be able to interact with its inhabitants and experiment with dialogue options and choices. Once the masterminds behind the attack on Eros catch up to them, they will also experience the combat and upgrade mechanics first hand. Owlcat said it aims to incorporate “valuable player feedback” from the beta into the final game.

“Since the beta is a work in progress, performance may vary and won’t reflect the final quality of the game,” Owlcat stressed. “On consoles, playing on Xbox Series X or PlayStation 5 is recommended.” That’s a clear nod to the underpowered nature of the Xbox Series S — hopefully The Expanse: Osiris Reborn will end up running well enough on the console.

In case you missed it, here's everything announced at Xbox Partner Preview March 2026.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/the-expanse-osiris-reborn-gets-a-debut-gameplay-trailer-with-big-mass-effect-vibes-a-closed-beta-and-a-spring-2027-release-window
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Stalker 2: Cost of Hope Is 'A Massive Nonlinear Expansion Packed With Dozens of Hours of Gameplay'
item.content:encoded:
The middle chapter of the "second trilogy."

Survival horror first-person shooter Stalker 2 is getting its first major story expansion this summer. As revealed at the Xbox Partner Preview showcase, Stalker 2: Cost of Hope is coming to PC, Xbox Series X and S, and PlayStation 5 and brings with it “dozens of hours of gameplay.”

Here’s the official blurb:

Step into a fresh expedition across the Zone — a massive nonlinear expansion packed with dozens of hours of gameplay. Expect haunting stories, twisted characters, and that same heavy atmosphere of decay… with just a flicker of hope breaking through.
It’s time to step into a new chapter of a well-known conflict. Duty, one of the oldest factions, sees the Zone as a threat that must be contained and destroyed. On the other side, Freedom believes it’s a gift — something to explore and harness for the greater good. For a time, this tension was held together by a fragile peace. But deals like that rarely last.

In Cost of Hope, you once again take on the role of Skif, the protagonist of the core game. After installing the DLC, a signal on the PDA will trigger the new storyline as the game unfolds. Developer GSC Game World said your choices shape what happens next.

Cost of Hope introduces two new regions to explore: “Navigate the iconic Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant, once abandoned and locked away for decades, now calling stalkers back in. Traverse the Iron Forest’s maze-like paths and uncover uncharted locations along the way. Each region features its own hub, quests, and activities. New weapons and gear give Skif a fighting chance against mutants, anomalies, and other dangers that are lurking.”

S.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2: Cost of Hope Screenshots

GSC Game World said Stalker 2 expansions will be released in installments, with Cost of Hope serving as the middle chapter of the “second trilogy.” That confirms there’s a future story DLC — details of which will be revealed later — and it will form a new, expansive narrative arc within the Stalker saga.

Stalker 2 and Cost of Hope are of course being developed amid Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, which is ongoing. Stalker 2 launched in 2024 and was a hit for GSC Game World, with over 6 million players.

In case you missed it, here's everything announced at Xbox Partner Preview March 2026.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/stalker-2-cost-of-hope-is-a-massive-nonlinear-expansion-packed-with-dozens-of-hours-of-gameplay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Alien Deathstorm, a New First-Person Survival Horror Game From Sniper Elite and Atomfall Studio Rebellion Revealed
item.content:encoded:
While there were a few things we knew to expect from today's Xbox Partner Preview event, like a new look at Stranger Than Heaven and the reveal of a new STALKER 2 expansion, there was a lot we didn't know about before the show. Like the reveal of Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish, or that Sniper Elite and Atomfall developer Rebellion's next game would be a first-person, sci-fi survival horror game called Alien Deathstorm.

The trailer (which you can see below) begins with the game's protagonist, who Rebellion is mysteriously keeping unnamed for now, only referring to them as "The Combat Engineer" in the Xbox Wire blog post that went live alongside the new trailer, wandering around what appears to be some kind of destroyed station. As the trailer cuts between different snippets of them exploring this abandoned area, we see one of the game's major threats in this monstrous alien creature that chases you around.

But by the end of the trailer, we get a look at what is clearly the main threat to be wary of, the massive storms that can tear apart whole buildings with deadly wind speeds. It's quite the spectacle and definitely lives up to the name, at least at first glance, of 'Alien Deathstorm.'

Ben Fisher, head of design at Rebellion, dug a bit more into what's going on in Alien Deathstorm and where the studio took its inspiration from. Firstly, what Fisher and Rebellion are willing to say about the game's plot and what you're doing in this game so far is "the player has been dispatched to this remote, off-world facility as a result of a complete loss of communications. Their job is to determine what has suddenly caused the colony to fall silent, and then try to save as many lives as possible."

A pretty vague and mysterious setting, but when Fisher digs into the game's inspirations, that description makes a bit more sense. "We’ve been inspired by the slightly more analogue world of 1980s sci-fi, which is much more mechanical keyboard than it is touchscreen. We think it’s given our colony a chunky, lived-in aesthetic that has a genuinely unique feel. We’ve taken influences from everything from Lovecraftian eldritch horror to 1980s VHS sci-fi, so we think that people will be intrigued by what we’ve built."

Fisher goes on to describe the game as a "true hybrid" between "high-tension exploration and discovery" gameplay and the kind of action-shooter gameplay the studio is already known for. "If we had to describe it as anything it’s an ‘action horror’ game with a layer of constant threat and environmental discovery that fully supports the second-to-second ballistic action," says Fisher.

Fisher also adds that he believes the combination of a shooter and a survival horror is "under-explored," and that Alien Deathstorm will stand out from other shooters or survival horror titles because of its hybrid nature. We'll see how right about that he is when Alien Deathstorm arrives on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S sometime in 2027.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/alien-deathstorm-xbox-partner-preview-reveal-rebellion-sniper-elite/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nutmeg is a deckbuilding football management game with a cute spin on the beautiful game
item.content:encoded:
The beautiful card game, I should say

It would be a reasonable assumption to make that there are only so many variations on football games you can make. Ultimately, no matter how many bells, whistles, or rocket powered cars you throw at it, it always comes down to getting a ball in a net and shouting SCOOOOOORE. Except in the case of Nutmeg!, a "nostalgic deckbuilding football manager" which looks like it features more faxing than any sort of kicking, but I mean that in an endearing way, promise.

In Nutmeg, you are the gaffer (the head honcho, the manager, the coach for the non-Brits out there) of a football team. The ultimate goal is take your team on an apparently 20-year-long journey throughout 1980s and '90s England, and presumably win a few matches. There are two modes to playthrough: Broadcast, which is more casual and sees a season wrapped up in around 90 minutes across 10 games, and Hardcore, which ups the match count to 50.

Then there's the main card game itself. When clicking on your deck, you're presented with your current action, and three potential actions. Cards come with an "Us" or "Them" signifier which tells you whether the move is directed towards your own team or the opposing one, with the most favourable outcome for your team listed at the bottom, and the most favourable for your opponent at the top. Each card has a certain chance of being put into play, all of which can be altered by using boost cards. For example, you might play a card called Goal Rush to increase the chances of scoring.

There's a lot more to it than that too, but perhaps it's a tad too in the weeds to explain every last detail. But you can also train up your team players, make money via merchandise and sponsorships, and even make transfers - that's where the fax comes in. It's such a detailed looking card game that if it weren't for the aspects like training, I could see it as a real, physical trading card game. Maybe Topps Now cards could do with a rival!

In any case, Nutmeg looks grand for those of you that like football but feel the need for something a bit fresher and less about running around some grass. And it's out today! You can pick it up on Steam right here.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/nutmeg-is-a-deckbuilding-football-management-game-with-a-cute-spin-on-the-beautiful-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 17 JRPGs You Can Add To Your Collection For Cheap During All Of The Spring Game Sales
item.content:encoded:
Spring is officially here and a bunch of games are discounted to celebrate, including some of my favorite JRPGs.

The below list is pulled from current PlayStation and Nintendo eShop sales, so all of the games should be suitable pickups whether you’re playing on Switch 1, Switch 2, PS4, or PS5. A bunch of the games that have PC versions are also on sale on Steam, and may be even cheaper there.

Here’s over a dozen games worth picking up if you don’t have them yet:

  • Digimon Story Time Stranger – $49 (30 percent off)
  • Octopath Traveler 0 – $37.50 (25 percent off)
  • Persona 3 Reload – $24 (60 percent off)
  • Dragon Quest 3 HD-2D Remake – $36 (40 percent off)
  • The Legend of Heroes: Trails of Cold Steel – $20 (50 percent off)
  • Star Ocean The Second Story R – $25 (50 percent off)
  • Fantasian Neo Dimension – $25 (50 percent off)
  • Dungeon Encounters – $9 (70 percent off)
  • Final Fantasy VII Rebirth – $30 (40 percent off)
  • Final Fantasy XVI – $25 (50 percent off)
  • Final Fantasy Tactics – The Ivalice Chronicles – $35 (30 percent off)
  • NieR:Automata The End of YoRHa Edition – $16 (60 percent off)
  • Voice of Cards – $25 (50 percent off)
  • Collection of Mana – $16 (60 percent off)
  • Sea of Stars – $21 (40 percent off)
  • Atelier Marie Remake – $25 (50 percent off)
  • Dragon Quest XI – $20 (50 percent off)

I tried to stick to great JRPGs that are actually great deals right now, meaning at least around 50 percent off. Final Fantasy Tactics, Digimon Story Time Stranger, and Octopath Traveler 0 are very recent releases, so they aren’t quite as cheap. Still, 30 percent off isn’t bad and each of them would be toward the higher end of my ranking of the above picks. Collection of Mana is a personal favorite around this time of year, and unfortunately Visions of Mana, the recent series reboot, isn’t on sale.

These are all digital downloads, but a handful of JRPGs are also deeply discounted in physical form at the moment thanks to spring sales at Amazon and a few other places. The boxed version of Final Fantasy Tactics is only $25 for PS5 on Woot. The Switch physical version of Pokémon Legends: Z-A is only $45. And if you own a Switch 2 and somehow still haven’t played Final Fantasy VII Remake Intergrade, it’s already on sale for just $30 a month after launch (game key card only, though).



https://kotaku.com/17-jrpgs-you-can-add-to-your-collection-for-cheap-during-all-of-the-spring-game-sales-2000682252
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Battlefield 6 Devs Used Multiple AI Tools During Development
item.content:encoded:
Electronic Arts used several well-known AI tools to accelerate the development of Battlefield 6.

Fast Company recently touted a list of innovative gaming companies for 2026. Nintendo, Embark Studios (makers of ARC Raiders), and Electronic Arts were included.

When discussing Electronic Arts, it is noted that a key part of developing Battlefield 6 was the Voice2Face software. This is a speech-to-animation system that converts recorded dialogue into high-quality, lip-synced facial animation. For all of the game’s cutscenes, EA reportedly used the technology for blocking animation. Additionally, 30% of the final animated speech was created using the AI software.

AI Was Also Used for Character Model Adjustments in Battlefield 6

Furthermore, the article states that Electronic Arts used FaceRig to accelerate character face creation. It’s noted that EA integrated FaceRig with Autodesk’s Maya and Flow, reducing a process that used to take approximately 2 weeks to only hours.

Battlefield does not disclose its use of AI on its Steam page, which is currently required for all games on the platform.

Battlefield has been a very successful game. In fact, EA saw a near 40% increase in its year-over-year net bookings, largely due to the success of the game at the tail end of 2025. Additionally, the game was named the best-selling title of 2025. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about this news concerning the use of AI tools in the development of Battlefield 6? Where do you think the line is regarding egregious use of AI in game development? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/battlefield-6-devs-used-multiple-ai-tools-during-development/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Eternal Life of Goldman coming to Game Pass on day one; Xbox Series demo now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Xbox Series

Full game due out in 2026.

Hand-drawn side-scrolling platformer The Eternal Life of Goldman will be available via Game Pass on day one when it launches later in 2026, publisher THQ Nordic and developer Weappy announced. The game is slated for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store. An Xbox Series demo is available now.

Here are the latest details, via THQ Nordic:

“What a ridiculous old man!” Goldman, hero of the upcoming hand-drawn 2D platforming adventure The Eternal Life of Goldman, has come to save the archipelago and to become the hero that the people there expected the least. While he may not seem like your typical savior-of-the-world type, Goldman has quite a few qualities that make him perfectly suitable for the job.

One might think Goldman can’t defeat anyone with that funny little cane, but please believe us: Yes, he cane! Make sure to check out the latest trailer to see all the unbelievable options this simple cane can give an old man! From jumping to the highest platforms—or onto the heads of his enemies—to hanging onto objects, pulling them, creating a flash of phosphorus, or turning it into a proper shield against enemy attacks: this cane is a powerful weapon in the hands of any old man who knows how to use it.

In the latest trailer, just shown at the Xbox Partner Preview Showcase earlier today, The Eternal Life of Goldman has dropped the big news: Players will be able to play The Eternal Life of Goldman day one with Xbox Game Pass!

And while the Game Pass announcement is already a reason to celebrate, Xbox players have another very good reason to get excited right now: you can check out the demo for The Eternal Life of Goldman on Xbox Series today!

Watch a new trailer below.

Game Pass Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/the-eternal-life-of-goldman-coming-to-game-pass-on-day-one-xbox-series-demo-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 17th century Korea-set dark action game MOOSA: Dirty Fate announced for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
About

“MOOSA” is a Korean word referring to a warrior who has mastered martial arts and chosen the path of the warrior.

In MOOSA: Dirty Fate, warriors driven by different ambitions and convictions collide. Their choices lead to blood—and destiny.

17th Century Joseon, A Land Turned Hell

This is an action game set in 17th century Korea. The 17th century was marked worldwide by the Little Ice Age an era of extreme climate anomalies, great famine, and devastating epidemics.

Korea was no exception. Historical records include the chilling line:

“With nothing left to eat, man ate man.”

Starvation and despair. Corrupt power and a collapsed order. An age that truly resembled hell.

Amid this chaos, you become “Gunn” a warrior who has lost everything to vengeance. You will battle demons disguised as humans and the forces that follow them, while uncovering the truth behind the legendary IMUGI.

Now, only one thing remains to you, Reclaim your life. The only one you can trust is yourself.

Experience 17th Century Joseon Dynasty

Breathtaking mountains and rivers, once-prosperous cities and centers of power, slums shattered by famine and shadowed back alleys.

Each region carries its own atmosphere, combat structure, and story. The world is not merely a backdrop. It is steeped in the lives and despair of those who endured that era. Every step you take becomes part of a legend.

“IMUGI.” Monster? God? or Tragedy?

“Is the Imugi a monster, a god… or another tragedy born of humanity?”

Even within the cruelty of demons lie hidden stories. Conversations with survivors, scattered battlefield records, and the dying words of fallen enemies. Piece by piece, the truth slowly emerges.

Sword and Bow, Your Heart

In the heat of battle, the sword and bow are more than weapons. They are your very heart.

Face overwhelming hordes in large-scale combat, engage in intense one-on-one boss battles against colossal creatures drawn from Korean folklore, and duel master swordsmen in stylish, skill-driven encounters.

MOOSA delivers a diverse and tightly crafted combat experience.

Victory in battle is survival itself the proof of your will.

The bow, in particular, introduces a distinct combat style that sets MOOSA apart from traditional action games. Historically mastered by the Korean people, the bow is reborn here as a strategic weapon capable of turning the tide of battle.

Combat is brutal. Choices are merciless. Only those who survive may write the next chapter.

Watch the announcement trailer below.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/17th-century-korea-set-dark-action-game-moosa-dirty-fate-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Criminal Underworlds, Detective Robots, And Vomiting Frogs: Everything We Saw In Xbox’s March Showcase
item.content:encoded:
March’s Xbox Partner Preview has come and gone, and we’ve been bombarded by news on 19 games coming soon to Xbox (and presumably, given Microsoft’s current habits, every other console, too). The headlines are Hades 2 coming to Xbox and PS5 next month, footage of the intriguing Stranger Than Heaven, and the news that games have run out of titles. Oh, and old men are the new protagonist du jour.

You can watch the entire presentation via the video below, or if that sounds like a lot, scroll down through our breakdown of every game mentioned and the essential information.

Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish

First up was Teyon’s follow-up to RoboCop: Rogue City, Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish, which began with a deeply sinister cop revealing his true nature as his eyes darkened and he attacked a woman tied to a chair. We then switch to those brave enough to hunt such vampiric darkness, with extra-stabby crucifixes and the like. Tabletop RPG Hunter: The Reckoning was first made into a game in 2002, but it’s been a while since we’ve heard from the license. This time it’s an action-RPG rather than a hack-and-slash game, and it’s due in the summer of 2027.

Wuthering Waves

Astonishingly enormous guns and scalpels abound in free-to-play gacha game Wuthering Waves, which finally makes its way to Xbox after launching on PC and mobile in 2024, then PS5 in 2025. This is coming to Game Pass, but then it would have been free anyway…

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn

Owlcat Games brings us a cover shooter set in the sci-fi world of The Expanse, the long-running TV show about the life of those on the outer rims of galactic exploration. There’s to be a closed beta in April, with more details about how to sign up over on Xbox Wire. It’s coming to Game Pass too.

Grave Seasons

Delightful pixel graphics and a hint of farming sim belie the truth about this game from horror supremos Blumhouse. It’s more fun if you just watch the trailer for yourself. This is coming to Game Pass on August 14.

Serious Sam: Shatterverse

Serious Sam is back, but apparently in some sort of meta-universe madness as five different Sams from previous spin-offs get together to fight the endless hordes the series is so famous for, but this time in five-player co-op. It’s due sometime this year.

Stranger Than Heaven

An extraordinarily violent trailer for the deeply intriguing Stranger Than Heaven, a tale of the Japanese crime underworld set across multiple parts of the 20th century from Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio. May 6 will apparently bring us more details about the game in an Xbox Direct. It’s not currently listed for Game Pass, but give the attention Microsoft is giving it, I imagine it’s desired.

Super Meat Boy 3D

The monstrously hard 3D platformer is demoed by showing a man losing his mind and destroying his apartment in fury at his constant failure. Which is fair enough. This long-awaited third entry in the Super Meat Boy series is due out as soon as March 31, and will be on Game Pass too.

Forever Ago

Get all lovey-duvvy and scrapbooky in this tale of an older man in a small town, which looked so very quaint and wholesome until the old dude apparently fell to his death through a broken flight of steps. This Annapurna-published game got what is called a “re-announcement trailer,” and is due on Game Pass in the fall.

Ascend to Zero

A frantic-looking action game where you can stop time to better attack your enemies, Ascend to Zero looks utterly brilliant in my opinion. Not long to wait as it’s out July 13, and it’ll join Game Pass, so there’s no need to pay extra to figure out what’s going on.

Dispatch

What else can I add? Dispatch is coming to Xbox this summer.

Alien Deathstorm

Developer Rebellion returns following 2025’s Atomfall with an FPS horror game that looks like it owes a lot to FEAR, and with a name that’s even more hilariously on the nose. Alien Deathstorm is what you’d call a game in a crime procedural about a kid who gets addicted to gaming. Seriously, was there nothing better? It’s memorable, I suppose, and let’s not forget this is the studio behind Sniper Elite: Nazi Zombie Army. It’s going to be on Game Pass and out sometime in 2027.

Frog Squad

It’s a co-op game about vomiting frogs. I have nothing else to add, other than that it’s coming to Game Pass and out in June.

Bluey’s Happy Snaps

The world-conquering doggy returns in a new game that looks like it’s about…everything! There’s photography, running around, coloring in, and, clearly, the original voice actors. It’s out in the fall and will be on Game Pass.

Stalker 2: Cost of Hope

The first major expansion to Stalker 2, Cost of Hope, was announced. It’s due this summer, a couple of years on from the core game’s much-delayed 2024 release. Unfortunately, this trailer was almost entirely cutscene, and very little game footage.

Moosa: Dirty Fate

Another barrage of cutscenes, this time for third-person slice-em-up Moosa: Dirty Fate. Which is another very bad name. “Dirty Fate” sounds like the worst possible euphemism. This is set in feudal Korea, which makes for an interesting change, and is being made by Iggymob, who are behind Gungrave GORE. It’s not out until 2027, but it will be coming to Game Pass.

The Eternal Life of Goldman

A beautiful-looking 2D platformer in which an old chap appears to craft ever-more-powerful canes with which to attack stunningly animated enemies. It’s not entirely clear what the narrator has against the dude, but he seems pretty awesome to us. This is quite a change of direction, and a very welcome one, for Weappy Studio, who previously gave us the missable This Is the Police games in the late 2010s. There’s a demo out today!

Vaunted

A third-person tactical shooter from first-timers Lost Lake Games that looks quite Borderlands-brained, but seemingly all in Russian. This could be an interesting mix of genres, where apparently each of the three characters has a different perspective on the story being told, and the narrative will adapt if either of the other two, sticking on as companions, gets killed. It’s due sometime this year and will be on Game Pass.

Hades II

Surprise! The colossally popular action-roguelite will be available next month on consoles, on April 14 to be exact, and more excitingly, on Game Pass.

Artificial Detective

“All of humanity has disappeared. Sounds like the biggest missing person case of all time?” That’s a good line. It seems we’re playing a detective robot in a world infested by some sort of creepy modular evil-bots, in a third-person shooter that looks really neat! Unlike its title. Artificial Detective? What is going on, developers? Anyway, indie team Vivix has got until 2027 to think of something better.



https://kotaku.com/xbox-partner-preview-hades-2-stranger-than-heaven-2000682251
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Frog Sqwad launches in June for Xbox Series, PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Xbox Series

Slapstick physics-based cooperative extraction puzzle platformer dated.

Slapstick physics-based cooperative extraction puzzle platformer Frog Sqwad will launch for Xbox Series and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store in June, developer Panic Stations announced. It will also be available via Game Pass.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Storm The Sewers for the glory of the Swamp King! Swing, jump, catapult your friends, and eat eat EAT until you grow into a humongous Megafrog. A cooperative extraction puzzle platformer for up to eight players. Squad up with your frog friends for a friendslop party in this slapstick physics adventure!

Team Up With Friends

Join up to seven froggy friends in a physics-based slapstick bonanza. Use your sticky tongue to swing from pipes, solve physics puzzles, drag giant food through winding tunnels and catapult your friends, all for the glory of the ravenous Swamp King!

Find Food

Plunge into ever-changing Sewers full of hazards, weird creatures, creative challenges—and delicious snacks. Chomp down and grow bigger than your teammates. Eat enough and you’ll swell into a round, rolling Megafrog!

…And Don’t Get Eaten

The Swamp King’s hunger grows with each passing day. Spend your well-earned Gold from successful runs on new physics toys and gadgets to give you an edge on future missions. Just make sure to meet your quotas, or you might end up as the Swamp King’s next meal!

Key Features

  • Lick Everything – Use your tongue to lick your friends, swing from pipes, grab physics objects and pull them around, and even catapult things!
  • Extraction Loop – Hop into The Sewers with your frog friends, gather food, and return to The Swamp to feed the Swamp King.
  • Daily Food Quota – Make sure to hit the daily food quota to pass the day and satisfy the Swamp King.
  • Experiment With Physics – Use anything you find in The Sewers to help you solve physics-based puzzles and platforming challenges.
  • Enemies – The Sewers are a strange place, filled with all kinds of unusual creatures – be on the look out!
  • Megafrog – As you eat food, your frog will grow in size. Eat enough food to transform into a MASSIVE Megafrog with a BIG BOOMING VOICE. Roll around The Sewers in style.
  • Frogspawn – If you take damage, you’ll vomit up any food you’ve eaten – take damage on an empty stomach, and you’ll transform into a pathetic frogspawn. You’ll need a friend to help feed you.
  • Voice Chat – Have a laugh with your friends over proximity chat while you play – your frog’s mouth will animate as you talk.

We Think You’ll Love Frog Sqwad If You Enjoy…

RV There Yet?, YAPYAP, PEAK, R.E.P.O., Content Warning, Lethal Company, Fall Guys, and more!

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Window Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/frog-sqwad-launches-in-june-for-xbox-series-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Donkey Kong Bananza has a big-brained approach to asset recycling
item.content:encoded:
Game developers seem to be cautiously opening up about re-using assets once again. In the last year, developers like former Far Cry and Assassin's Creed director Alex Hutchison, Elden Ring Nightreign director Junya Ishizaki, and IO Interactive CEO Hakan Abrak have all been vocal about why re-using game assets (be they 3D models, sound effects, or other creations) is important for sustainable game design. This candor is notable because for years, certain groups of players have loudly complained when encountering re-used models—but that backlash has been surpassed by the need to make huge games at a more sustainable pace.

Even Donkey Kong Bananza developer Nintendo seems to be coming out of its shell when it comes to discussing this topic. In a 2026 GDC Festival of Gaming presentation that covered the studio's process of building destructible voxel environments, programmer Tatsuya Kurihara showed off a pipeline created by his team that allowed Bananza's art team to quickly create destructible objects that had been sculpted using 3D animation.

Related:How voxels enabled a juicy gameplay loop in Donkey Kong Bananza

Kurihara's explanation of the pipeline was brief, using the head of Bananza antagonist Void Kong as an example. He explained that models like Void Kong's head could be created in Maya, converted into voxel data in Houdini, and then "voxelized" at runtime in Bananza to create polygons. This wasn't used for Void Kong himself, but rather to create a destructible piece of terrain sculpted in the shape of Void Kong's head, akin to the carvings of four US presidents on Mt. Rushmore (also known by the Sioux Nation—which maintains its territorial claim to the mountain—as "Six Grandmothers"). 

The creation acts both as a shrine to Void Kong's ego—and as a punching bag for players to channel their frustration out on the pint-sized corporate villain. "The shapes of items and enemies, as well as iconic terrain within levels are all created using this system," Kurihara said.

Looking at the model of Kong and its voxelized in-game counterpart—it raised the question whether Void Kong's character model had been repurposed for this stone effigy.

In a follow-up interview with Kurihara and producer Kenta Motokura, the programmer declined to say if Void Kong's head was an example of asset recycling—but he did confirm Nintendo used the Maya-to-Houdini pipeline to take previously-made 3D assets and reuse them for voxel environments.

Donkey Kong Bananza's voxelization pipeline was a request from the art team

According to Kurihara, the voxelization process came later in Bananza's development, after the programming team gave the Bananza artists and designers tools to make their own voxel creations. "That leads to a situation where the artists or the level designer will start chugging away, and they'll create this complex voxel design that even I, as the creator, [would say] 'I didn't know you could create something so complicated in this level editor.'"

"It got me thinking—'well, what kind of features can I add to make this process easier?'"

Kurihara said after working with the voxel creation tools, they came back to the engineering team and said "we want to be able to actually 'shave' voxels in this state,' and then we implemented a tool to be able to do that." 

Artists then could then either create 3D assets explicitly meant to be voxelized, or take existing 3D assets used for non-voxelized characters or objects and convert them into a destructible form. 

Slide via Nintendo, photo by the author

It's not the reuse itself that's notable in this instance, but rather how this method for re-use expands the lifecycle of a 3D model beyond its original conception. Asset recycling is conventionally understood as the process of using assets across multiple games—like how the robotic monsters in Horizon Forbidden West re-used animations created for Horizon Zero Dawn with almost zero modification.

More expansive modification might be used in a game itself. The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim for instance, famously created varied bookshelf sizes sometimes just by sticking existing bookshelf models in the floor to create end tables, reducing the number of assets needed to fill out indoor environments.

Bananza's reuse process expands that idea even further, creating a method that allows objects and characters to be manifested as destructible terrain. Now these objects don't just exist for mere aesthetics, they become part of the core gameplay as well.

And considering these objects were not "meant" to be destroyed, they overlapped with a design principle that emerged as iteration progressed in Bananza's development: that it's more satisfying to destroy objects that don't look like they should be destroyed.

Or as Kurihara stated plainly—drawing laughter from the packed room in San Francisco—"It is more fun to destroy that which is beautiful."

Game Developer and GDC Festival of Gaming are sibling organizations under Informa Festivals.

About the Author

Senior Editor, GameDeveloper.com

Bryant Francis is a writer, journalist, and narrative designer based in Boston, MA. He currently writes for Game Developer, a leading B2B publication for the video game industry. His credits include Proxy Studios' 4X strategy game Zephon, Iron Anchor Studios' Down With The Ship, and Amplitude Studio's 2017 game Endless Space 2.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/art/donkey-kong-bananza-has-a-big-brained-approach-to-asset-recycling
